specs.section_path: >  > Prediction II: Causal Entropic Force Signatures in Quantum Randomness > Proposed Experimental Protocol: Correlating QRNG Statistical Deviations with Collective Coherence Metrics > Falsification Condition
source_formulae: P0R05229: The theory is falsified if no statistically significant correlation is found between the QRNG deviation metric and the collective coherence metric, or if any observed correlation can be attributed to environmental artefacts (e.g., electromagnetic leakage) not controlled for by the shielding.
P0R05230: [IMAGE:Ein Bild, das Text, Screenshot, Schrift, Reihe enthlt. KI-generierte Inhalte knnen fehlerhaft sein.]
P0R05231: Fig.: Data Analysis Protocol. This flowchart provides a step-by-step overview of the data processing pipeline, from raw data streams to the final statistical test of the core hypothesis.

P0R05232: Input -> Physiology -> QRNG -> Statistics -> Falsification, stacked top-to-bottom: From raw QRNG bitstream and multi-participant ECG/EEG, compute a group coherence metric C(t)C(t)C(t) (HRV coherence, inter-subject EEG PLV -> average) and a randomness deviation D(t)D(t)D(t) (windowed NIST STS p-values -> K-S vs. Uniform(0,1)). Test the coupling via cross-correlation C(t)D(t)C(t)\leftrightarrow D(t)C(t)D(t) with lag scans. Falsify if no significant correlation remains after controls.
